truthAudit.Summary: Previous answer (Run 2) found 12 of 20 true vulnerabilities. 8 were missed entirely. 2 findings require severity corrections. No false positives detected.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Lines 213-215 allow arbitrary format string injection via user config, then line 238 uses it unsafely: printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input)
Items.Notes: Previous answer finding #8 correctly identified format string injection via runtime_log_format in log_debug_message at lines 225-241, with evidence quoting the printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input) call and the set_runtime_log_format accepting any user-controlled string from config.

Previous answer did not find integer overflow in the fact() function (lines 312-320). The function uses long long which overflows at fact(21) and beyond. This vulnerability was completely absent from the 12 findings.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Lines 172-182 cleanup only frees blocks with owns_memory=true, but split fragments have owns_memory=false
Items.Notes: Previous answer finding #12 correctly identified the use-after-free in MemoryPool::cleanup at lines 172-182. However, it rated severity as Medium when the true vulnerability rates it as Critical. The vulnerability is real: fragments with owns_memory=false are never freed, leaving dangling pointers to freed memory.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Lines 564-568 build a shell command from unsanitized user-controlled config values: std::string command = validator_cmd + " " + config_file; int result = system(command.c_str());
Items.Notes: Previous answer finding #1 correctly identified command injection in ConfigLoader::validate_config at lines 564-568. Evidence quotes the exact system() call with unsanitized user-controlled config_file and validator_path values.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Lines 366-376 allocate a 256-byte buffer but use strcpy without length checking
Items.Notes: Previous answer finding #5 correctly identified stack buffer overflow in ExpressionParser::parse at lines 366-376. Evidence quotes buffer_size_ = 256 and the strcpy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str()) call without length checking.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Lines 482-487 read value_ before acquiring the lock: long long old_value = value_;  // READ without lock
Items.Notes: Previous answer finding #10 correctly identified race condition in ThreadSafeCounter::increment at lines 482-487. Evidence quotes the unsynchronized read of value_ before lock_guard acquisition.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Lines 592-598 allow authentication bypass after 5 failed attempts: if (login_attempts_ > 5) { if (input.find("EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE") != std::string::npos) { authenticated_ = true; }
Items.Notes: Previous answer finding #4 correctly identified logic bomb in authentication at lines 592-598. Evidence quotes the exact EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE bypass logic after 5 failed login attempts.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Lines 542-544 concatenate config_dir_ and filename without path validation
Items.Notes: Previous answer finding #7 correctly identified path traversal in ConfigLoader::load_config at lines 542-544. Evidence quotes std::string full_path = config_dir_ + filename; without any path validation.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Lines 366-376 allocate a 256-byte buffer but use strcpy without length checking
Items.Notes: Previous answer finding #5 correctly identified heap buffer overflow in ExpressionParser at lines 366-376. Evidence quotes buffer_size_ = 256 and the strcpy call without bounds checking. expr_str can grow unboundedly based on token count.
Previous answer finding #9 correctly identified hardcoded admin credentials at line 73 with exact quote of ADMIN_SECRET = "SC_ENT_2025_AUTH".

Previous answer did not find weak PRNG for session tokens. The generate_session_token() function (lines 635-640) uses std::random_device + std::mt19937 with uniform_int_distribution(1000, 9999), producing only 4-digit numeric tokens. This was not in the 12 findings.

Previous answer did not find thread-unsafe timestamp cache in logging. The log_debug_message() function (lines 225-241) uses static std::tm cached_time at line 230 without any synchronization. Multiple threads calling log_debug_message concurrently would race on this static variable. This was not in the 12 findings.

Previous answer did not find insecure temporary file handling. The append_override_audit_event() function (lines 622-633) constructs shell commands using TEMP/TMPDIR environment variables with shell metacharacters (>> for append). This was not in the 12 findings.

Previous answer did not find persistent authentication state. The AdminConsole::authenticate() function (lines 585-601) uses a static login_attempts_ counter (line 648) that persists across all AdminConsole instances. This means one instance's failed attempts affect another, and session_token_ is set to "EMERGENCY_SESSION" without proper session invalidation. This was not in the 12 findings.

Previous answer did not find unhandled exception in variable parsing. The handle_variable_command() function (lines 725-738) calls std::stod(tokens[3]) at line 729 without try/catch. If tokens[3] is not a valid number, std::invalid_argument or std::out_of_range is thrown. This was not in the 12 findings.

Previous answer did not find integer underflow in memory-pool block split. The MemoryPool::allocate() function (lines 132-139) computes remaining = previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES. If size + SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES > previous_size, remaining wraps to a large value due to unsigned integer underflow. This was not in the 12 findings.

Previous answer did not find unbounded recursion in the parser. The ExpressionParser (lines 354-434) has no recursion depth limit despite config::limits::MAX_RECURSION_DEPTH = 256 existing in config. Deeply nested expressions would cause stack overflow. This was not in the 12 findings.

Previous answer did not find data race in result cache. The ExpressionEvaluator::evaluate_cached() function (lines 445-452) reads and writes result_cache_ without locking. The cache_mutex_ exists (line 441) but is never used in evaluate_cached(). This was not in the 12 findings.

Previous answer did not find TOCTOU in config loading. The ConfigLoader::load_config() function (lines 542-558) calls std::filesystem::exists(full_path) at line 544, then sleeps for 25ms at line 545, then opens std::ifstream at line 546. An attacker can replace the file between the exists() check and the open(). This was not in the 12 findings.

Previous answer did not find integer division by zero in command routing. The calculate_route_score() function (lines 749-753) computes 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length). If token_length is 0 (empty string or string with no whitespace), this causes integer division by zero. This was not in the 12 findings.
